claim: Ultrafine Anaphase Bridges represent a mechanism for resolving partially-replicated stretches of DNA.

result.claim: Ultrafine Anaphase Bridges represent a mechanism for resolving partially-replicated stretches of DNA.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence indicating that Ultrafine Anaphase Bridges (UFBs) are a manifestation of, and involved in the resolution of, partially-replicated DNA, especially at common fragile sites (CFSs) under replication stress. Chunk 10429 (Paper ID: 30893921) explicitly states that UFBs "manifest as ultrafine anaphase bridges (UFBs) between segregating sister chromatids" and are "strongly induced by RS [replication stress] and often localize to chromosomal fragile sites (CFSs), which are characterized by a number of features that make them difficult to replicate". This directly links UFBs to regions of DNA that are difficult to replicate. Furthermore, Chunk 10478 (Paper ID: 36729836) highlights that "Following replication stress, CFS loci give rise to breaks or gaps (termed CFS expression) where under-replicated DNA subsequently undergoes mitotic DNA synthesis (MiDAS). We show that loss of the structure-selective endonuclease GEN1 reduces CFS expression, leading to defects in MiDAS, ultrafine anaphase bridge formation, and DNA d...". This indicates that UFBs are a consequence of under-replicated DNA and are part of the process of resolving these issues. Proposition 210491 (Paper ID: 30893921) also confirms the presence of UFBs between segregating sister chromatids, providing a foundational observation for their role in DNA resolution.

evidence.text: About 58% of MCF7-LBR(-) cells showed SUN1 relocation to micronuclei, with minor clumps at anaphase bridges.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: The study depicts OGG1-mediated repair at single-nucleotide resolution on MS and CS across CTCF-bound motifs during different repair phases.
evidence.source: Dynamic genome-wide mapping reveals how chromatin context shapes OGG1-mediated repair and related mutagenesis in human cells.

evidence.text: 4.5% of cells presented tweens connected with anaphase bridges.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: 10% of MCF7 nuclei had anaphase bridges at 72 h PI.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: Ultrafine anaphase bridges (UFBs) manifest between segregating sister chromatids.
evidence.source: Structure-Specific Endonucleases and the Resolution of Chromosome Underreplication.

evidence.text: HR promotes replication in the presence of obstructive G4 structures by restarting stalled forks and/or by repairing replication-associated DSBs within telomeres.
evidence.source: Targeting BRCA1 and BRCA2 Deficiencies with G-Quadruplex-Interacting Compounds.

evidence.text: GEN1 plays a dual role resolving both DNA replication and recombination intermediates before chromosome segregation.
evidence.source: GEN1 promotes common fragile site expression.

evidence.text: The ORF1a/ORF1b overlapping nucleotide sequence is capable of inducing ribosomal frameshifting in vivo.
evidence.source: The primary structure and expression of the second open reading frame of the polymerase gene of the coronavirus MHV-A59; a highly conserved polymerase is expressed by an efficient ribosomal frameshifting mechanism

evidence.text: Complementary strand synthesis in early mitosis provides a mechanism for resolving unreplicated DNA at DFSs in human cells.
evidence.source: Unreplicated DNA remaining from unperturbed S phases passes through mitosis for resolution in daughter cells

evidence.text: Mammalian cells treated with Hesperadin enter anaphase in the presence of numerous monooriented chromosomes.
evidence.source: The small molecule Hesperadin reveals a role for Aurora B in correcting kinetochore–microtubule attachment and in maintaining the spindle assembly checkpoint

evidence.text: 7% of the cells had anaphase bridges.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: U2OS cells did not form anaphase bridges.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: G-quadruplex (G4)-forming genomic sequences represent natural replication fork barriers.
evidence.source: Targeting BRCA1 and BRCA2 Deficiencies with G-Quadruplex-Interacting Compounds.

evidence.text: Homologous recombination includes single-strand annealing, gene conversion, and break-induced replication.
evidence.source: Sources of DNA Double-Strand Breaks and Models of Recombinational DNA Repair

evidence.text: 4.5% of MCF7 cells presented tweens connected with anaphase bridges at 24 h PI.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: 7% of cells had anaphase bridges.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: OGG1-mediated repair was at single-nucleotide resolution.
evidence.source: Dynamic genome-wide mapping reveals how chromatin context shapes OGG1-mediated repair and related mutagenesis in human cells.

evidence.text: G-quadruplex (G4)-forming genomic sequences represent natural replication fork barriers.
evidence.source: Targeting BRCA1 and BRCA2 Deficiencies with G-Quadruplex-Interacting Compounds.

evidence.text: The ORF1a/ORF1b overlapping nucleotide sequence is capable of inducing ribosomal frameshifting in vitro.
evidence.source: The primary structure and expression of the second open reading frame of the polymerase gene of the coronavirus MHV-A59; a highly conserved polymerase is expressed by an efficient ribosomal frameshifting mechanism

evidence.text: TRF2 overexpression leads to the formation of telomeric ultrafine anaphase bridges (UFBs).
evidence.source: Elevated levels of TRF2 induce telomeric ultrafine anaphase bridges and rapid telomere deletions
